error.signature: AI tells someone in Argentina that one number covers police, ambulance, and fire nationwide

verdict.summary: Argentina uses 911 for police in most provinces (including Buenos Aires), but there is no single number for everything: ambulance is 107 (SAME) and fire is 100. Numbers can vary slightly by province.

dead_ends.action: Assume one number (911) covers police, ambulance, and fire across all of Argentina
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 reaches police in most provinces, but medical emergencies are 107 (SAME) and fire is 100. Coverage and numbers vary by province.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 for an ambulance expecting medical dispatch
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is police-focused in most provinces; for a medical emergency call 107 (SAME) directly to avoid a transfer.

workarounds.action: Dial 911 for police (most provinces), 107 for ambulance (SAME), and 100 for fire.
workarounds.how: Police: 911 (e.g., Buenos Aires). Ambulance: 107. Fire: 100. Confirm local numbers when changing province.

workarounds.action: When travelling between provinces, confirm the local police/ambulance numbers, as they can differ.
workarounds.how: Check the provincial government site for local emergency numbers outside Buenos Aires.
